tasks.model_response:
1. What changed

I rebuilt the static board shell in [ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L791) and [ui.css](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.css#L43) so the page now renders as a centered, constrained application surface instead of a full-width stack. The hero now uses the `Blackdog Backlog` eyebrow, keeps the copy directly under the project name, moves the stats into the hero, separates workspace metadata from artifact links, and gives the top-right last-rendered stamp stronger visual weight ([ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L793), [ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L1083)).

I also moved search/filter controls into the execution-map panel and turned the body into a three-panel layout: execution map, completed-task history, and inbox. The old recent-results rail now renders completed-task history cards derived from finished tasks, while keeping existing task card content and detail dialogs intact ([ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L820), [ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L1250), [ui.css](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.css#L126), [ui.css](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.css#L593)).

2. Why

This matches the task objective and evidence: narrower shell, clearer hero hierarchy, stronger render status, stats inside the hero, and a visually distinct completed-task history. The layout now reads as a focused backlog surface rather than a generic wide report page.

3. Validation performed

I ran `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'` twice after the final CSS pass; both runs passed (`Ran 36 tests ... OK`).

I also rendered the real board with `./.VE/bin/blackdog render --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --actor supervisor/child-01` and verified the resulting HTML visually via a headless Chrome screenshot of `/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/backlog-index.html`.

I recorded the structured task result and committed the branch changes as `ffbee04` (`Rebuild board shell hero layout`).

4. Residual risks or assumptions

Completed-task history is driven by tasks whose current snapshot status is `complete`; a task with a recorded result but no completed state will not appear there. I did not add or change Python tests because the existing render/snapshot suite already covered the touched renderer path and stayed green.
tasks.model_response_html:
1. What changed

I rebuilt the static board shell in ui.py and ui.css so the page now renders as a centered, constrained application surface instead of a full-width stack. The hero now uses the Blackdog Backlog eyebrow, keeps the copy directly under the project name, moves the stats into the hero, separates workspace metadata from artifact links, and gives the top-right last-rendered stamp stronger visual weight (ui.py, ui.py).

I also moved search/filter controls into the execution-map panel and turned the body into a three-panel layout: execution map, completed-task history, and inbox. The old recent-results rail now renders completed-task history cards derived from finished tasks, while keeping existing task card content and detail dialogs intact (ui.py, ui.py, ui.css, ui.css).

  1. Why

This matches the task objective and evidence: narrower shell, clearer hero hierarchy, stronger render status, stats inside the hero, and a visually distinct completed-task history. The layout now reads as a focused backlog surface rather than a generic wide report page.

  1. Validation performed

I ran PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' twice after the final CSS pass; both runs passed (Ran 36 tests ... OK).

I also rendered the real board with ./.VE/bin/blackdog render --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --actor supervisor/child-01 and verified the resulting HTML visually via a headless Chrome screenshot of /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/backlog-index.html.

I recorded the structured task result and committed the branch changes as ffbee04 (Rebuild board shell hero layout).

  1. Residual risks or assumptions

Completed-task history is driven by tasks whose current snapshot status is complete; a task with a recorded result but no completed state will not appear there. I did not add or change Python tests because the existing render/snapshot suite already covered the touched renderer path and stayed green.

tasks.latest_result_preview: Adjusted hero snapshot freshness and task timing rendering: content_updated_at now tracks latest backlog event timestamp; last_activity reused as computed; summarizeTimeOnTask now preserves live timing segment; added/updated regression coverage in tests and refreshed docs for CLI/output semantics.
latest_result_what_changed: Adjusted hero snapshot freshness and task timing rendering: content_updated_at now tracks latest backlog event timestamp; last_activity reused as computed; summarizeTimeOnTask now preserves live timing segment; added/updated regression coverage in tests and refreshed docs for CLI/output semantics.
tasks.model_response:
Done. I completed and recorded work for `BLACK-17bb2df5c3` on branch `agent/black-17bb2df5c3-fix-hero-task-timing-and-freshness-summary-db2ad9c0`.

### What changed
- Updated `src/blackdog/ui.py`:
  - `build_ui_snapshot` now sets `content_updated_at` from the latest event timestamp (fallback to `generated_at`), instead of always mirroring `generated_at`.
  - Kept `last_checked_at` as the freshness-check heartbeat timestamp and avoids conflating it with content freshness.
  - Reused computed `last_activity` value consistently for snapshot output.
  - Updated hero timing summary JS (`summarizeTimeOnTask`) to preserve the `live` segment when present, so active task timing remains visible.
- Updated tests in `tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`:
  - Added/adjusted snapshot freshness assertions so `content_updated_at` and `last_checked_at` can differ correctly.
  - Added regression checks verifying hero time-on-task includes `live` during in-progress activity.
  - Added explicit freshness assertions around rendered `Last content updated` vs `Last checked`.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/FILE_FORMATS.md`: clarified snapshot timestamp semantics.
  - `docs/CLI.md`: clarified `blackdog snapshot` timestamp field meanings.
  - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: clarified freshness source separation in UI status architecture.

### Why
- Root cause was that `content_updated_at` was incorrectly set to `generated_at`, making content freshness indistinguishable from check freshness.
- Hero helper logic filtered too aggressively and dropped `live` timing text, making task-time rendering effectively static during active work.
- This patch restores live distinction and keeps the hero strip truthful during active work.

### Validation
- Committed as:
  - `f584e28`  
  - Commit message: `Fix hero timing freshness and content update tracking`
- Ran test suite earlier in this task: `make test` → `Ran 66 tests in 148.809s` / `OK`.
- Result recorded via child protocol:  
  `/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/task-results/BLACK-17bb2df5c3/20260319-153225-0bd55aef.json`

### Residual risks / assumptions
- No additional runtime behavior changes expected outside hero and snapshot freshness contracts.
- If snapshot consumers relied on `content_updated_at == generated_at`, they should now adapt to the corrected semantics (latest event timestamp).

- Updated [docs/INTEGRATION.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-17bec9ea0e-hard-gate-primary-worktree-implementation-in-blackdog-s-repo-contract-81ffaf1e/docs/INTEGRATION.md#L29) to route adopters toward `doc_routing_defaults`, restate the hard gate in setup flow, and explain that generated skills should surface both the worktree contract and required review docs.
- Updated [src/blackdog/scaffold.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-17bec9ea0e-hard-gate-primary-worktree-implementation-in-blackdog-s-repo-contract-81ffaf1e/src/blackdog/scaffold.py#L214) so generated `SKILL.md` files now:

tasks.model_response:
Completed and recorded.

### 1) What changed

- **Supervisor contract now carries explicit landing outcome fields**
  - Added `branch_ahead` and `landed` to `ChildRun` state and to child payloads/events.
  - Included these fields in `child_finish` payload and supervisor `run` output payloads.
  - Files:
    - [src/blackdog/supervisor.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/src/blackdog/supervisor.py)

- **Snapshot/UI rendering now distinguishes landed/no-op/failed-to-land**
  - Captures `branch_ahead`, `landed`, and `land_error` in run artifact rows.
  - Adds `latest_run_branch_ahead`, `latest_run_landed`, `latest_run_land_error` into task rows.
  - Renders a `Landed` chip (with commit link) when landed.
  - Renders `Failed to land` chip for blocked, non-complete tasks when branch was ahead and did not land.
  - Suppresses that failed chip once task is complete (keeps completed-task behavior clean).
  - Allows linked chips in dialog/card rendering.
  - Files:
    - [src/blackdog/ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/src/blackdog/ui.py)
    - [src/blackdog/ui.css](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/src/blackdog/ui.css)

- **Tests for new behavior**
  - Added no-op completion test ensuring no landed badge appears.
  - Extended blocked landing test to assert failed-to-land operator status/chip.
  - Extended completed-task snapshot assertions for landed badge + commit link on completed landed task.
  - Files:
    - [tests/test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py)

- **Docs updated for contract**
  - Added landing outcome semantics and outcome fields in run/event/task snapshots.
  - Files:
    - [docs/CLI.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/docs/CLI.md)
    - [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
    - [docs/FILE_FORMATS.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/docs/FILE_FORMATS.md)

### 2) Why

- This encodes three explicit completion outcomes consistently:
  - Successful land → linked `Landed` badge.
  - No-op completion → no landed badge.
  - Blocked land with pending branch work ahead → `Failed to land` failure signal that blocks downstream flow.

### 3) Validation performed

- Ran full test target: `make test` (passes, 42 tests).
- Additional direct runs used while iterating:
  - `./.VE/bin/python -m unittest ...` for focused tests around completed/no-op/blocked snapshots.

### 4) Residual risks / assumptions

- `Failed to land` chip is intentionally suppressed on tasks that are already complete to preserve stale-run filtering behavior seen in existing UI expectations.
- Chip link styling now includes `.chip-landed` to avoid unstyled/undefined chip class behavior for commit-linked landed status.

### Result reporting / commit

- Commit: `45ff9f8`
- Blackdog result recorded:
  - `./.VE/bin/blackdog result record --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --id BLACK-4da2cb16fd --actor supervisor/child-02 --status success ...`

- Updated `tests/test_blackdog_cli.py` (second-round fixture additions and fixes).
  - Kept the new second-round integration flow (`test_supervise_run_performs_a_second_round_convergence_optimization_funnel`) on top of the existing convergence fixture.
  - Reused the first-round launcher semantics via `install_model_backed_supervisor_launcher`.
  - Added second-round tasks:
    - two optimization tasks depending on first-round winner
    - one final chooser task depending on those optimizations
  - Added assertions for:
    - both-round task execution via `supervise run`
    - convergence winner artifacts (`convergence-round1-winner.txt`, `convergence-round2-winner.txt`)
    - final chooser validation/validation fields
    - event log (`supervisor_run_sweep`) and removed task sweep semantics
    - completed-task sweep and board snapshot status checks
- Kept the existing first-round helper robustness fix in the embedded launcher:
  - task title parsing now tolerates indentation via `^\s*Title:\s*...`.
- Fixed score parsing regex in embedded launcher to use valid raw-digit patterns (no escaped backslash over-escape in code strings).
- Removed dependence on `run_dir/winner.json` in first-round result validation and kept final deterministic assertions from recorded result payloads instead.
- Adjusted first-round status expectation in the new second-round test to accept evaluator terminal state as either `done` or `released` (stable behavior observed for evaluator tasks), while keeping round-1 candidate tasks strict to `done`.

Why:
- The objective requires a staged convergence funnel with a second optimization round feeding a final chooser.
- These assertions now validate exactly that behavioral pipeline and remain resilient to release behavior of non-committable evaluator tasks.

tasks.latest_result_preview: Rerendered backlog-index.html from supervisor child lifecycle events so active child launches and finishes refresh the static view immediately.

tasks.model_response:
1. What changed: I added a supervisor-side render hook in [supervisor.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-8f9dc27a24-refresh-the-static-backlog-index-during-active-supervisor-runs-5b53ed3b/src/blackdog/supervisor.py#L126) and call it on `child_launch_failed`, `child_launch`, and `child_finish` so `backlog-index.html` refreshes while a child is actively running instead of only after the loop cycle ends. I also added HTML snapshot test helpers in [test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-8f9dc27a24-refresh-the-static-backlog-index-during-active-supervisor-runs-5b53ed3b/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py#L80) plus a gated regression in [test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-8f9dc27a24-refresh-the-static-backlog-index-during-active-supervisor-runs-5b53ed3b/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py#L2634) that proves the embedded static snapshot flips to `running` before the child is released. Commit: `3dd9667`.

2. Why: the loop previously rerendered only before/after a cycle, so a long-running child left the static page stale mid-cycle. Rendering on child lifecycle transitions makes the operator view show active work as soon as the child launches and refresh again on failure/finish.

3. Validation: ran `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_blackdog_cli.BlackdogCliTests.test_supervise_loop_refreshes_static_html_while_child_is_running tests.test_blackdog_cli.BlackdogCliTests.test_supervise_run_launches_child_command_in_worktree tests.test_blackdog_cli.BlackdogCliTests.test_supervise_loop_runs_multiple_cycles` and `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'` (27 tests, pass). I also recorded the structured result with Blackdog for `BLACK-8f9dc27a24`.

4. Residual risks or assumptions: this refreshes on launch failure, launch, and finish only. It does not add periodic heartbeat rerenders during a long-running child between those lifecycle events.

tasks.model_response:
Implemented.

### 1) What changed

- Added bootstrap-time AGENTS scaffolding when absent.
  - File: [`src/blackdog/scaffold.py`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-9683a63dcc-scaffold-a-baseline-agents-md-when-bootstrapping-a-repo-that-lacks-agent-docs-33e6f5c3/src/blackdog/scaffold.py)
  - New helper `_ensure_baseline_agents_file(...)` writes a minimal `AGENTS.md` when missing, and is invoked from `bootstrap_project(...)`.
  - Existing `AGENTS.md` is preserved; bootstrap will not overwrite it.

- Added regression coverage for the new behavior.
  - File: [`tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-9683a63dcc-scaffold-a-baseline-agents-md-when-bootstrapping-a-repo-that-lacks-agent-docs-33e6f5c3/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py)
  - New test `test_bootstrap_writes_baseline_agents_md_only_if_missing` verifies:
    - `AGENTS.md` is created on first bootstrap.
    - The bootstraped baseline contains expected marker content.
    - A pre-existing `AGENTS.md` is not replaced on subsequent bootstrap.

- Updated host onboarding docs to reflect new bootstrap behavior.
  - Files:
    - [`README.md`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-9683a63dcc-scaffold-a-baseline-agents-md-when-bootstrapping-a-repo-that-lacks-agent-docs-33e6f5c3/README.md)
    - [`docs/INTEGRATION.md`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-9683a63dcc-scaffold-a-baseline-agents-md-when-bootstrapping-a-repo-that-lacks-agent-docs-33e6f5c3/docs/INTEGRATION.md)

### 2) Why

`bootstrap_project` is the one-command host repo onboarding path and previously left repos without any AGENT guidance if they were blank. This ensures a first-time repo gets a minimal baseline contract automatically, while avoiding accidental overwrite of existing host-specific instructions.

### 3) Validation performed

- Ran:
  - `make test`
- Result:
  - `Ran 53 tests in 134.124s`
  - `OK`
- Recorded task result via child helper:
  - [`/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/child?`](/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/task-results/BLACK-9683a63dcc/20260319-101723-5adc0fb8.json) (generated by `blackdog-child result record`, status success, validation `make test`)

### 4) Residual risks / assumptions

- The baseline `AGENTS.md` template is intentionally minimal and generic.
- `make test` output includes existing `SyntaxWarning` messages in unrelated test paths (pre-existing).
- Changes are committed on branch:
  - `f776fd0`

tasks.model_response:
Implemented and committed on task branch `agent/black-a8c9394420-render-explicit-board-timing-metrics-in-fixed-header-order-db2ad9c0`.

### What changed
- Reworked hero timing snapshot generation to explicit fields:
  - `[src/blackdog/ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-a8c9394420-render-explicit-board-timing-metrics-in-fixed-header-order-db2ad9c0/src/blackdog/ui.py)`
    - Removed freeform `_time_on_task_summary`.
    - Added explicit `hero_highlights` fields:
      - `time_since_last_check`
      - `time_since_last_update`
      - `total_time_on_sweep`
      - `total_time_on_backlog`
    - Added `_latest_event_at(...)` and `_duration_label(...)` helpers.
    - Updated `build_ui_snapshot(...)` call wiring to pass `events`, `content_updated_at`, `last_checked_at`, `generated_at`.
    - Updated JS render template hero line to exactly:
      - `Time since last check`
      - `Time since last update`
      - `Total time on sweep`
      - `Total time on backlog`
    - Removed now-obsolete JS helpers `relativeTimeFromNow` and `summarizeTimeOnTask`.

- Updated tests to lock the new contract and order checks:
  - `[tests/test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-a8c9394420-render-explicit-board-timing-metrics-in-fixed-header-order-db2ad9c0/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py)`
    - Replaced legacy `time_on_task` and old label assertions with new explicit hero fields.
    - Added assertions that rendered HTML contains the four new labels.
    - Added assertions on fixed render order for the four timing items in `renderMetaItem(...)`.
    - Removed stale `part.includes("live")` assertion.

- Updated docs for snapshot contract:
  - `[docs/FILE_FORMATS.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-a8c9394420-render-explicit-board-timing-metrics-in-fixed-header-order-db2ad9c0/docs/FILE_FORMATS.md)`
    - Replaced `hero_highlights.time_on_task` with four fixed timing keys.
    - Updated control panel wording to fixed timing line.
  - `[docs/CLI.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-a8c9394420-render-explicit-board-timing-metrics-in-fixed-header-order-db2ad9c0/docs/CLI.md)`
    - `blackdog snapshot` contract now explicitly lists the four hero timing keys.

### Why
- Required stable hero display is now driven by deterministic snapshot fields rather than a computed freeform summary, and the front-end renders those metrics in a fixed order.

### Validation
- Ran required check:
  - `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'`
  - Result: `Ran 66 tests in 159.432s` and `OK`.
- Recorded protocol result:
  - `.../blackdog-child result record --status success --what-changed "..."`
  - Wrote result at:
    `/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/task-results/BLACK-a8c9394420/20260319-154441-a537f9b3.json`

### Commit
- `44e97ab` — Render fixed hero timing metrics in board header order

### Residual assumptions
- “Total time on backlog” is computed from the oldest available snapshot event (or `content_updated_at/generated_at` fallback), which matches current event-based ordering logic used by this task; if you want a narrower definition (e.g., based only on active/sweep-related events), that can be tightened in a follow-up slice.

tasks.model_response:
Implemented and committed on task branch.

### What changed
- Added reusable model-backed supervisor launcher helper in `tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`:
  - New `install_model_backed_supervisor_launcher(...)` writes a provided `codex` launcher and rewrites `blackdog.toml` `launch_command`.
  - Includes low-effort tuning via `--effort low` when enabled (default), while preserving sandbox-bypass behavior.
- Added new end-to-end convergence test in the same file:
  - `test_supervise_run_selects_best_convergence_candidate_via_model_backed_children`
  - Seeds 4 parallel implementation tasks + 1 evaluator task in wave 0.
  - Uses real supervisor execution (`blackdog supervise run` with `--format json`), no fake launcher path.
  - Verifies coherent traces:
    - all 5 task IDs launched
    - launch command includes `--effort`
    - each task reaches `task_claims[*]["status"] == "done"`
    - events include `task_result` and `child_finish`
    - task results present for all implementations + evaluator
    - evaluator-selected winner matches highest candidate score and `winner.json` payload
    - HTML snapshot shows all tasks completed with `success`.

### Why
- This implements the requested safe first slice: reusable fixture helper plus a single-round convergence scenario that exercises the real model-backed supervisor flow and dependent evaluator selection behavior.

### Validation performed
- `python3 -m py_compile tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`
- Blackdog result recorded:
  - `/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/task-results/BLACK-b3ae66aed8/20260318-003134-a88df662.json`

### Residual risks / assumptions
- I did **not** run `make test` (or suite-level test execution); only compile-time check plus the scoped change was validated.
- Convergence scoring logic in the embedded launcher is fixture-specific and intentionally simplistic (`Candidate 1..4` fixed score map).

Commit on task branch:
- `e30bfe7` ([tests/test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b3ae66aed8-create-a-model-backed-convergence-fixture-for-release-semantics-ff899573/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py))

tasks.model_response:
Implemented and committed: `536aab7`

What changed
- Added structured child-run telemetry capture to supervisor launch/prompt flow in [src/blackdog/supervisor.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b65c668772-add-structured-child-run-telemetry-and-prompt-metadata-to-supervisor-artifacts-36b2a460/src/blackdog/supervisor.py):
  - Added fixed child prompt template + hash/version constants and switched `_build_child_prompt()` to template formatting.
  - Added launch telemetry builder with:
    - `launch_command`
    - `launch_command_strategy`
    - `prompt_template_version`
    - `prompt_template_hash`
    - `prompt_hash`
  - Added `telemetry` on `ChildRun`.
  - Threaded launch strategy through command resolution and child launch args.
  - Wrote/ persisted run-level metadata artifact in `<supervisor_run>/<task_id>/metadata.json`.
  - Included telemetry in `child_finish` event payload.
  - Added telemetry into `record_task_result(...)` metadata for supervisor-produced results, and when the child already wrote a result, the latest child result file is updated to include telemetry metadata.
- Extended result recording schema in [src/blackdog/store.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b65c668772-add-structured-child-run-telemetry-and-prompt-metadata-to-supervisor-artifacts-36b2a460/src/blackdog/store.py):
  - `record_task_result(...)` now accepts optional `metadata` and writes it into result payload.
- Updated test coverage in [tests/test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b65c668772-add-structured-child-run-telemetry-and-prompt-metadata-to-supervisor-artifacts-36b2a460/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py):
  - Fixed broken assertions in `test_supervise_run_launches_child_command_in_worktree`.
  - Added checks for `metadata.json` fields and `child_finish` payload telemetry fields.
- Documented the new artifact contract in [docs/FILE_FORMATS.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b65c668772-add-structured-child-run-telemetry-and-prompt-metadata-to-supervisor-artifacts-36b2a460/docs/FILE_FORMATS.md):
  - `child_finish` payload keys now include telemetry fields.
  - `task-results` entries now include `metadata`.
  - Added `supervisor-runs/*/<task-id>/metadata.json` format description.

Why
- To measure child-run startup friction, launch strategy, and prompt immutability, and to make task results machine-queryable without inferring from raw logs/stdout.

Validation performed
- `make test` (full suite): passed (48 tests).
- Additional focused regression check after final patch:
  - `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_blackdog_cli.BlackdogCliTests.test_supervise_run_launches_child_command_in_worktree`: passed.
- Result recorded as requested:
  - `./.VE/bin/blackdog result record --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --id BLACK-b65c668772 --actor supervisor/child-01 ...`

Residual risks / assumptions
- If a child writes multiple result files in one run, only the newest newly-created result file is metadata-updated.
- `run_id` in `metadata.json` is the supervisor run id (not the result filename-derived run id); that remains by design and should be treated as sibling identifiers with different semantics.
- Child-provided metadata from existing older or non-supervisor result rows is preserved and augmented, not replaced.
tasks.model_response_html:
Implemented and committed: 536aab7

What changed

  • Added structured child-run telemetry capture to supervisor launch/prompt flow in src/blackdog/supervisor.py:
  • Added fixed child prompt template + hash/version constants and switched _build_child_prompt() to template formatting.
  • Added launch telemetry builder with:
  • launch_command
  • launch_command_strategy
  • prompt_template_version
  • prompt_template_hash
  • prompt_hash
  • Added telemetry on ChildRun.
  • Threaded launch strategy through command resolution and child launch args.
  • Wrote/ persisted run-level metadata artifact in <supervisor_run>/<task_id>/metadata.json.
  • Included telemetry in child_finish event payload.
  • Added telemetry into record_task_result(...) metadata for supervisor-produced results, and when the child already wrote a result, the latest child result file is updated to include telemetry metadata.
  • Extended result recording schema in src/blackdog/store.py:
  • record_task_result(...) now accepts optional metadata and writes it into result payload.
  • Updated test coverage in tests/testblackdogcli.py:
  • Fixed broken assertions in test_supervise_run_launches_child_command_in_worktree.
  • Added checks for metadata.json fields and child_finish payload telemetry fields.
  • Documented the new artifact contract in docs/FILE_FORMATS.md:
  • child_finish payload keys now include telemetry fields.
  • task-results entries now include metadata.
  • Added supervisor-runs/*/<task-id>/metadata.json format description.

Why

  • To measure child-run startup friction, launch strategy, and prompt immutability, and to make task results machine-queryable without inferring from raw logs/stdout.

Validation performed

  • make test (full suite): passed (48 tests).
  • Additional focused regression check after final patch:
  • PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_blackdog_cli.BlackdogCliTests.test_supervise_run_launches_child_command_in_worktree: passed.
  • Result recorded as requested:
  • ./.VE/bin/blackdog result record --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --id BLACK-b65c668772 --actor supervisor/child-01 ...

Residual risks / assumptions

  • If a child writes multiple result files in one run, only the newest newly-created result file is metadata-updated.
  • run_id in metadata.json is the supervisor run id (not the result filename-derived run id); that remains by design and should be treated as sibling identifiers with different semantics.
  • Child-provided metadata from existing older or non-supervisor result rows is preserved and augmented, not replaced.

tasks.model_response:
Done. Task `BLACK-e982d3b588` has been completed and protocol-released.

1. What changed
- `src/blackdog/ui.py`: completed-task chip row is now wrapped in a `.result-chips` container for right alignment, and board snapshot now emits:
  - `generated_at`
  - `content_updated_at` (alias of generated time)
  - `last_checked_at` (derived from latest `supervisor_runs_dir/*/status.json`)
- `src/blackdog/ui.css`: added styling for `.result-top` and `.result-chips` to right-align completed outcome chips while preserving responsive behavior.
- `docs/FILE_FORMATS.md`: documented new snapshot fields `content_updated_at` and `last_checked_at`.
- `docs/CLI.md`: updated `blackdog snapshot` output contract to include both freshness fields.
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: noted freshness metadata in static snapshot payload.
- `tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`:
  - added assertions for new timestamp fields in snapshot contract
  - added regression test validating `last_checked_at` from supervisor status timestamps
  - updated static HTML text assertions to `"Last content updated"` / `"Last checked"`
  - added coverage for `class="result-chips"` in completed card rendering

2. Why
- Fixes the visual drift by right-justifying completed-task outcome chips.
- Makes board metadata trustworthy by exposing both content freshness and last supervisor check time in one snapshot payload and docs/tests.

3. Validation performed
- Task branch committed as `492dde6`.
- `make test` passed in this branch (`Ran 56 tests` with `OK`).
- Branch protocol output was recorded and released via child helper:
  - `blackdog-child result record --status success ...`
  - `blackdog-child release --note ...`  
    Returned task id: `BLACK-e982d3b588`.

4. Residual risks / assumptions
- `last_checked_at` currently picks the latest timestamp across all status files in `supervisor_runs_dir/*/status.json`; if non-supervisor actors write status files there, filtering may be needed later.
- `content_updated_at` is explicitly set equal to `generated_at` for now to preserve existing snapshot timing semantics while adding explicit naming.

tasks.model_response:
Implemented and committed on branch `agent/black-ed60a6b503-add-a-child-safe-blackdog-protocol-wrapper-and-simplify-supervisor-child-prompts-36b2a460`.

Committed: `927c74f`

What changed

- Added a generated child-safe protocol helper script in the supervisor launch flow:
  - New constant/script template: [`src/blackdog/supervisor.py`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-ed60a6b503-add-a-child-safe-blackdog-protocol-wrapper-and-simplify-supervisor-child-prompts-36b2a460/src/blackdog/supervisor.py)
    - `CHILD_PROTOCOL_HELPER = "blackdog-child"`
    - New `_CHILD_PROTOCOL_HELPER_TEMPLATE`
    - New `_build_child_protocol_helper(...)` writing executable `blackdog-child` in each child run directory
    - `_launch_child_run(...)` now creates helper and injects its path into child prompt metadata
  - Child prompt updated to use env-prebound helper:
    - protocol path embedded as `protocol_command`
    - removed hardcoded `./.VE/bin/blackdog`/`blackdog` command usage
    - updated startup guidance to skip repeated manual startup/claim/complete steps
    - prompt version bumped to `2`

- Updated CLI env-default behavior for child ergonomics:
  - [`src/blackdog/cli.py`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-ed60a6b503-add-a-child-safe-blackdog-protocol-wrapper-and-simplify-supervisor-child-prompts-36b2a460/src/blackdog/cli.py)
    - Added `_env_default(...)` and `_env_required(...)`
    - `cmd_release` and `cmd_result_record` now resolve `--project-root`, task id, and agent/actor from env when omitted:
      - `BLACKDOG_PROJECT_ROOT`
      - `BLACKDOG_TASK_ID`
      - `BLACKDOG_AGENT_NAME`
    - `cmd_inbox_list` now resolves `--project-root` from `BLACKDOG_PROJECT_ROOT`; recipient optionally from `BLACKDOG_AGENT_NAME`
    - Parser updates:
      - `release`: `--id`/`--agent` no longer required
      - `result record`: `--id`/`--actor` no longer required

- Updated tests for new protocol behavior and prompt expectations:
  - [`tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-ed60a6b503-add-a-child-safe-blackdog-protocol-wrapper-and-simplify-supervisor-child-prompts-36b2a460/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py)
    - Updated direct calls to `_build_child_prompt` with new `protocol_command` parameter
    - Updated child prompt assertions to helper-based command paths
    - Adjusted inbox test to avoid env-dependent recipient filtering drift by passing explicit `--recipient supervisor` in resolved inbox listing

Why

- The child now gets a deterministic, workspace-local command entrypoint (`blackdog-child`) that bakes in task/project/agent context and chooses a usable Blackdog CLI path, eliminating repeated command probing and environment mistakes.
- CLI commands used by delegated children can now be less brittle by reading task/project/actor from environment, matching the new child run protocol.
- Prompt wording and contract metadata now explicitly steer children away from manual startup/claim/complete steps and toward the helper.

Validation performed

- Ran tests: `make test`
  - Result: `Ran 48 tests in 109.712s` `OK`
- Result recorded:
  - `cd /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog && PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m blackdog_cli result record --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --id BLACK-ed60a6b503 --actor supervisor/child-02 --status success --what-changed "Add child protocol helper wrapper and env-default CLI parsing for release/result/inbox list" --validation "make test"`
  - Result path emitted: `/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/task-results/BLACK-ed60a6b503/20260318-230044-db937cb4.json`

Residual risks / assumptions

- `cmd_inbox_list` now applies `BLACKDOG_AGENT_NAME` as default recipient when present; this is intentional for child runs but can hide messages for non-child sessions unless `--recipient` is explicitly set (tests adjusted accordingly).

tasks.model_response:
1. What changed

I rebuilt the static board shell in [ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L791) and [ui.css](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.css#L43) so the page now renders as a centered, constrained application surface instead of a full-width stack. The hero now uses the `Blackdog Backlog` eyebrow, keeps the copy directly under the project name, moves the stats into the hero, separates workspace metadata from artifact links, and gives the top-right last-rendered stamp stronger visual weight ([ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L793), [ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L1083)).

I also moved search/filter controls into the execution-map panel and turned the body into a three-panel layout: execution map, completed-task history, and inbox. The old recent-results rail now renders completed-task history cards derived from finished tasks, while keeping existing task card content and detail dialogs intact ([ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L820), [ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.py#L1250), [ui.css](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.css#L126), [ui.css](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-089552a2be-narrow-the-board-shell-and-rebuild-the-hero-panel-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/ui.css#L593)).

2. Why

This matches the task objective and evidence: narrower shell, clearer hero hierarchy, stronger render status, stats inside the hero, and a visually distinct completed-task history. The layout now reads as a focused backlog surface rather than a generic wide report page.

3. Validation performed

I ran `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'` twice after the final CSS pass; both runs passed (`Ran 36 tests ... OK`).

I also rendered the real board with `./.VE/bin/blackdog render --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --actor supervisor/child-01` and verified the resulting HTML visually via a headless Chrome screenshot of `/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/backlog-index.html`.

tasks.model_response:
Done. I completed and recorded work for `BLACK-17bb2df5c3` on branch `agent/black-17bb2df5c3-fix-hero-task-timing-and-freshness-summary-db2ad9c0`.

### What changed
- Updated `src/blackdog/ui.py`:
  - `build_ui_snapshot` now sets `content_updated_at` from the latest event timestamp (fallback to `generated_at`), instead of always mirroring `generated_at`.
  - Kept `last_checked_at` as the freshness-check heartbeat timestamp and avoids conflating it with content freshness.
  - Reused computed `last_activity` value consistently for snapshot output.
  - Updated hero timing summary JS (`summarizeTimeOnTask`) to preserve the `live` segment when present, so active task timing remains visible.
- Updated tests in `tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`:
  - Added/adjusted snapshot freshness assertions so `content_updated_at` and `last_checked_at` can differ correctly.
  - Added regression checks verifying hero time-on-task includes `live` during in-progress activity.
  - Added explicit freshness assertions around rendered `Last content updated` vs `Last checked`.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/FILE_FORMATS.md`: clarified snapshot timestamp semantics.
  - `docs/CLI.md`: clarified `blackdog snapshot` timestamp field meanings.
  - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: clarified freshness source separation in UI status architecture.

### Why
- Root cause was that `content_updated_at` was incorrectly set to `generated_at`, making content freshness indistinguishable from check freshness.
- Hero helper logic filtered too aggressively and dropped `live` timing text, making task-time rendering effectively static during active work.
- This patch restores live distinction and keeps the hero strip truthful during active work.

### Validation
- Committed as:
  - `f584e28`  
  - Commit message: `Fix hero timing freshness and content update tracking`
- Ran test suite earlier in this task: `make test` → `Ran 66 tests in 148.809s` / `OK`.
- Result recorded via child protocol:  
  `/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/task-results/BLACK-17bb2df5c3/20260319-153225-0bd55aef.json`

### Residual risks / assumptions
- No additional runtime behavior changes expected outside hero and snapshot freshness contracts.
- If snapshot consumers relied on `content_updated_at == generated_at`, they should now adapt to the corrected semantics (latest event timestamp).

Completed and recorded.

### 1) What changed

- **Supervisor contract now carries explicit landing outcome fields**
  - Added `branch_ahead` and `landed` to `ChildRun` state and to child payloads/events.
  - Included these fields in `child_finish` payload and supervisor `run` output payloads.
  - Files:
    - [src/blackdog/supervisor.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/src/blackdog/supervisor.py)

- **Snapshot/UI rendering now distinguishes landed/no-op/failed-to-land**
  - Captures `branch_ahead`, `landed`, and `land_error` in run artifact rows.
  - Adds `latest_run_branch_ahead`, `latest_run_landed`, `latest_run_land_error` into task rows.
  - Renders a `Landed` chip (with commit link) when landed.
  - Renders `Failed to land` chip for blocked, non-complete tasks when branch was ahead and did not land.
  - Suppresses that failed chip once task is complete (keeps completed-task behavior clean).
  - Allows linked chips in dialog/card rendering.
  - Files:
    - [src/blackdog/ui.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/src/blackdog/ui.py)
    - [src/blackdog/ui.css](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/src/blackdog/ui.css)

- **Tests for new behavior**
  - Added no-op completion test ensuring no landed badge appears.
  - Extended blocked landing test to assert failed-to-land operator status/chip.
  - Extended completed-task snapshot assertions for landed badge + commit link on completed landed task.
  - Files:
    - [tests/test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py)

- **Docs updated for contract**
  - Added landing outcome semantics and outcome fields in run/event/task snapshots.
  - Files:
    - [docs/CLI.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/docs/CLI.md)
    - [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
    - [docs/FILE_FORMATS.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-4da2cb16fd-encode-landed-task-outcomes-across-ui-docs-and-contracts-ff899573/docs/FILE_FORMATS.md)

### 2) Why

- This encodes three explicit completion outcomes consistently:
  - Successful land → linked `Landed` badge.
  - No-op completion → no landed badge.
  - Blocked land with pending branch work ahead → `Failed to land` failure signal that blocks downstream flow.

### 3) Validation performed

- Ran full test target: `make test` (passes, 42 tests).
- Additional direct runs used while iterating:
  - `./.VE/bin/python -m unittest ...` for focused tests around completed/no-op/blocked snapshots.

### 4) Residual risks / assumptions

- `Failed to land` chip is intentionally suppressed on tasks that are already complete to preserve stale-run filtering behavior seen in existing UI expectations.
- Chip link styling now includes `.chip-landed` to avoid unstyled/undefined chip class behavior for commit-linked landed status.

### Result reporting / commit

tasks.model_response:
Implemented and committed on branch `agent/black-5f2418ce79-extend-the-convergence-fixture-with-a-second-round-optimization-funnel-ff899573`.

What changed:
- Updated `tests/test_blackdog_cli.py` (second-round fixture additions and fixes).
  - Kept the new second-round integration flow (`test_supervise_run_performs_a_second_round_convergence_optimization_funnel`) on top of the existing convergence fixture.
  - Reused the first-round launcher semantics via `install_model_backed_supervisor_launcher`.
  - Added second-round tasks:
    - two optimization tasks depending on first-round winner
    - one final chooser task depending on those optimizations
  - Added assertions for:
    - both-round task execution via `supervise run`
    - convergence winner artifacts (`convergence-round1-winner.txt`, `convergence-round2-winner.txt`)
    - final chooser validation/validation fields
    - event log (`supervisor_run_sweep`) and removed task sweep semantics
    - completed-task sweep and board snapshot status checks
- Kept the existing first-round helper robustness fix in the embedded launcher:
  - task title parsing now tolerates indentation via `^\s*Title:\s*...`.
- Fixed score parsing regex in embedded launcher to use valid raw-digit patterns (no escaped backslash over-escape in code strings).
- Removed dependence on `run_dir/winner.json` in first-round result validation and kept final deterministic assertions from recorded result payloads instead.
- Adjusted first-round status expectation in the new second-round test to accept evaluator terminal state as either `done` or `released` (stable behavior observed for evaluator tasks), while keeping round-1 candidate tasks strict to `done`.

Why:
- The objective requires a staged convergence funnel with a second optimization round feeding a final chooser.
- These assertions now validate exactly that behavioral pipeline and remain resilient to release behavior of non-committable evaluator tasks.

Validation:
- No test suite run in this step.
- No local claim/completion action taken (per your task constraints).

1. What changed: I added a supervisor-side render hook in [supervisor.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-8f9dc27a24-refresh-the-static-backlog-index-during-active-supervisor-runs-5b53ed3b/src/blackdog/supervisor.py#L126) and call it on `child_launch_failed`, `child_launch`, and `child_finish` so `backlog-index.html` refreshes while a child is actively running instead of only after the loop cycle ends. I also added HTML snapshot test helpers in [test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-8f9dc27a24-refresh-the-static-backlog-index-during-active-supervisor-runs-5b53ed3b/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py#L80) plus a gated regression in [test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-8f9dc27a24-refresh-the-static-backlog-index-during-active-supervisor-runs-5b53ed3b/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py#L2634) that proves the embedded static snapshot flips to `running` before the child is released. Commit: `3dd9667`.

2. Why: the loop previously rerendered only before/after a cycle, so a long-running child left the static page stale mid-cycle. Rendering on child lifecycle transitions makes the operator view show active work as soon as the child launches and refresh again on failure/finish.

3. Validation: ran `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_blackdog_cli.BlackdogCliTests.test_supervise_loop_refreshes_static_html_while_child_is_running tests.test_blackdog_cli.BlackdogCliTests.test_supervise_run_launches_child_command_in_worktree tests.test_blackdog_cli.BlackdogCliTests.test_supervise_loop_runs_multiple_cycles` and `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'` (27 tests, pass). I also recorded the structured result with Blackdog for `BLACK-8f9dc27a24`.

4. Residual risks or assumptions: this refreshes on launch failure, launch, and finish only. It does not add periodic heartbeat rerenders during a long-running child between those lifecycle events.

    - Removed freeform `_time_on_task_summary`.
    - Added explicit `hero_highlights` fields:
      - `time_since_last_check`
      - `time_since_last_update`
      - `total_time_on_sweep`
      - `total_time_on_backlog`

    - Updated `build_ui_snapshot(...)` call wiring to pass `events`, `content_updated_at`, `last_checked_at`, `generated_at`.
    - Updated JS render template hero line to exactly:
      - `Time since last check`
      - `Time since last update`
      - `Total time on sweep`
      - `Total time on backlog`
    - Removed now-obsolete JS helpers `relativeTimeFromNow` and `summarizeTimeOnTask`.

- Updated tests to lock the new contract and order checks:
  - `[tests/test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-a8c9394420-render-explicit-board-timing-metrics-in-fixed-header-order-db2ad9c0/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py)`
    - Replaced legacy `time_on_task` and old label assertions with new explicit hero fields.
    - Added assertions that rendered HTML contains the four new labels.
    - Added assertions on fixed render order for the four timing items in `renderMetaItem(...)`.
    - Removed stale `part.includes("live")` assertion.

- Updated docs for snapshot contract:
  - `[docs/FILE_FORMATS.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-a8c9394420-render-explicit-board-timing-metrics-in-fixed-header-order-db2ad9c0/docs/FILE_FORMATS.md)`
    - Replaced `hero_highlights.time_on_task` with four fixed timing keys.
    - Updated control panel wording to fixed timing line.
  - `[docs/CLI.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-a8c9394420-render-explicit-board-timing-metrics-in-fixed-header-order-db2ad9c0/docs/CLI.md)`
    - `blackdog snapshot` contract now explicitly lists the four hero timing keys.

### Why
- Required stable hero display is now driven by deterministic snapshot fields rather than a computed freeform summary, and the front-end renders those metrics in a fixed order.

### Validation

- `44e97ab` — Render fixed hero timing metrics in board header order

- “Total time on backlog” is computed from the oldest available snapshot event (or `content_updated_at/generated_at` fallback), which matches current event-based ordering logic used by this task; if you want a narrower definition (e.g., based only on active/sweep-related events), that can be tightened in a follow-up slice.

Implemented and committed on task branch.

### What changed
- Added reusable model-backed supervisor launcher helper in `tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`:
  - New `install_model_backed_supervisor_launcher(...)` writes a provided `codex` launcher and rewrites `blackdog.toml` `launch_command`.
  - Includes low-effort tuning via `--effort low` when enabled (default), while preserving sandbox-bypass behavior.
- Added new end-to-end convergence test in the same file:
  - `test_supervise_run_selects_best_convergence_candidate_via_model_backed_children`
  - Seeds 4 parallel implementation tasks + 1 evaluator task in wave 0.
  - Uses real supervisor execution (`blackdog supervise run` with `--format json`), no fake launcher path.
  - Verifies coherent traces:
    - all 5 task IDs launched
    - launch command includes `--effort`
    - each task reaches `task_claims[*]["status"] == "done"`
    - events include `task_result` and `child_finish`
    - task results present for all implementations + evaluator
    - evaluator-selected winner matches highest candidate score and `winner.json` payload
    - HTML snapshot shows all tasks completed with `success`.

### Why
- This implements the requested safe first slice: reusable fixture helper plus a single-round convergence scenario that exercises the real model-backed supervisor flow and dependent evaluator selection behavior.

- `python3 -m py_compile tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`
- Blackdog result recorded:

- I did **not** run `make test` (or suite-level test execution); only compile-time check plus the scoped change was validated.
- Convergence scoring logic in the embedded launcher is fixture-specific and intentionally simplistic (`Candidate 1..4` fixed score map).

  - Included telemetry in `child_finish` event payload.

  - `record_task_result(...)` now accepts optional `metadata` and writes it into result payload.
- Updated test coverage in [tests/test_blackdog_cli.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b65c668772-add-structured-child-run-telemetry-and-prompt-metadata-to-supervisor-artifacts-36b2a460/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py):
  - Fixed broken assertions in `test_supervise_run_launches_child_command_in_worktree`.
  - Added checks for `metadata.json` fields and `child_finish` payload telemetry fields.
- Documented the new artifact contract in [docs/FILE_FORMATS.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b65c668772-add-structured-child-run-telemetry-and-prompt-metadata-to-supervisor-artifacts-36b2a460/docs/FILE_FORMATS.md):
  - `child_finish` payload keys now include telemetry fields.
  - `task-results` entries now include `metadata`.
  - Added `supervisor-runs/*/<task-id>/metadata.json` format description.

Why
- To measure child-run startup friction, launch strategy, and prompt immutability, and to make task results machine-queryable without inferring from raw logs/stdout.

Validation performed
- `make test` (full suite): passed (48 tests).
- Additional focused regression check after final patch:
  - `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_blackdog_cli.BlackdogCliTests.test_supervise_run_launches_child_command_in_worktree`: passed.
- Result recorded as requested:
  - `./.VE/bin/blackdog result record --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --id BLACK-b65c668772 --actor supervisor/child-01 ...`

Residual risks / assumptions
- If a child writes multiple result files in one run, only the newest newly-created result file is metadata-updated.
- `run_id` in `metadata.json` is the supervisor run id (not the result filename-derived run id); that remains by design and should be treated as sibling identifiers with different semantics.
- Child-provided metadata from existing older or non-supervisor result rows is preserved and augmented, not replaced.

Updated the board contract language in [docs/CLI.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b66a0af458-align-board-docs-and-snapshot-contract-with-the-new-layout-d62b1862/docs/CLI.md), [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b66a0af458-align-board-docs-and-snapshot-contract-with-the-new-layout-d62b1862/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md), and [docs/FILE_FORMATS.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b66a0af458-align-board-docs-and-snapshot-contract-with-the-new-layout-d62b1862/docs/FILE_FORMATS.md) so they describe the current three-panel board: hero metadata, `Backlog`, and `Completed Tasks`. The file-format doc now also calls out that `board_tasks` stays in the snapshot even when the browser filters completed rows out of the active backlog panel, and it documents the localized inbox-link behavior.

Updated the skill generator in [src/blackdog/scaffold.py](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b66a0af458-align-board-docs-and-snapshot-contract-with-the-new-layout-d62b1862/src/blackdog/scaffold.py) and the checked-in skill text in [.codex/skills/blackdog/SKILL.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-b66a0af458-align-board-docs-and-snapshot-contract-with-the-new-layout-d62b1862/.codex/skills/blackdog/SKILL.md) so generated agent guidance matches that same board contract. The task branch is committed at `b8f83e4`.

2. Why

The existing docs and skill text were still describing the older “execution map/top-right inbox” model. The current UI renders a narrower panelized board with hero workspace metadata, backlog-local controls, and completed-task history, so the written contract needed to match the implemented behavior and snapshot semantics.

3. Validation performed

Ran `make test` and it passed: 36 tests, `OK`.

Ran `git diff --check` with no issues.

Recorded the structured task result with `./.VE/bin/blackdog result record --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --id BLACK-b66a0af458 --actor supervisor/child-05 ...`.

4. Residual risks or assumptions

I updated the checked-in skill text manually instead of regenerating it from this task worktree, so repo-committed absolute paths stay unchanged instead of being rewritten to the worktree path. No runtime/UI behavior changed in this task; this is a contract-alignment update only.

tasks.model_response:
Done. Task `BLACK-e982d3b588` has been completed and protocol-released.

1. What changed
- `src/blackdog/ui.py`: completed-task chip row is now wrapped in a `.result-chips` container for right alignment, and board snapshot now emits:
  - `generated_at`
  - `content_updated_at` (alias of generated time)
  - `last_checked_at` (derived from latest `supervisor_runs_dir/*/status.json`)
- `src/blackdog/ui.css`: added styling for `.result-top` and `.result-chips` to right-align completed outcome chips while preserving responsive behavior.
- `docs/FILE_FORMATS.md`: documented new snapshot fields `content_updated_at` and `last_checked_at`.
- `docs/CLI.md`: updated `blackdog snapshot` output contract to include both freshness fields.
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: noted freshness metadata in static snapshot payload.
- `tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`:
  - added assertions for new timestamp fields in snapshot contract
  - added regression test validating `last_checked_at` from supervisor status timestamps
  - updated static HTML text assertions to `"Last content updated"` / `"Last checked"`
  - added coverage for `class="result-chips"` in completed card rendering

2. Why
- Fixes the visual drift by right-justifying completed-task outcome chips.
- Makes board metadata trustworthy by exposing both content freshness and last supervisor check time in one snapshot payload and docs/tests.

3. Validation performed
- Task branch committed as `492dde6`.
- `make test` passed in this branch (`Ran 56 tests` with `OK`).
- Branch protocol output was recorded and released via child helper:
  - `blackdog-child result record --status success ...`
  - `blackdog-child release --note ...`  
    Returned task id: `BLACK-e982d3b588`.

4. Residual risks / assumptions
- `last_checked_at` currently picks the latest timestamp across all status files in `supervisor_runs_dir/*/status.json`; if non-supervisor actors write status files there, filtering may be needed later.
- `content_updated_at` is explicitly set equal to `generated_at` for now to preserve existing snapshot timing semantics while adding explicit naming.

tasks.model_response:
Implemented and committed on branch `agent/black-ed60a6b503-add-a-child-safe-blackdog-protocol-wrapper-and-simplify-supervisor-child-prompts-36b2a460`.

Committed: `927c74f`

What changed

- Added a generated child-safe protocol helper script in the supervisor launch flow:
  - New constant/script template: [`src/blackdog/supervisor.py`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-ed60a6b503-add-a-child-safe-blackdog-protocol-wrapper-and-simplify-supervisor-child-prompts-36b2a460/src/blackdog/supervisor.py)
    - `CHILD_PROTOCOL_HELPER = "blackdog-child"`
    - New `_CHILD_PROTOCOL_HELPER_TEMPLATE`
    - New `_build_child_protocol_helper(...)` writing executable `blackdog-child` in each child run directory
    - `_launch_child_run(...)` now creates helper and injects its path into child prompt metadata
  - Child prompt updated to use env-prebound helper:
    - protocol path embedded as `protocol_command`
    - removed hardcoded `./.VE/bin/blackdog`/`blackdog` command usage
    - updated startup guidance to skip repeated manual startup/claim/complete steps
    - prompt version bumped to `2`

- Updated CLI env-default behavior for child ergonomics:
  - [`src/blackdog/cli.py`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-ed60a6b503-add-a-child-safe-blackdog-protocol-wrapper-and-simplify-supervisor-child-prompts-36b2a460/src/blackdog/cli.py)
    - Added `_env_default(...)` and `_env_required(...)`
    - `cmd_release` and `cmd_result_record` now resolve `--project-root`, task id, and agent/actor from env when omitted:
      - `BLACKDOG_PROJECT_ROOT`
      - `BLACKDOG_TASK_ID`
      - `BLACKDOG_AGENT_NAME`
    - `cmd_inbox_list` now resolves `--project-root` from `BLACKDOG_PROJECT_ROOT`; recipient optionally from `BLACKDOG_AGENT_NAME`
    - Parser updates:
      - `release`: `--id`/`--agent` no longer required
      - `result record`: `--id`/`--actor` no longer required

- Updated tests for new protocol behavior and prompt expectations:
  - [`tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-ed60a6b503-add-a-child-safe-blackdog-protocol-wrapper-and-simplify-supervisor-child-prompts-36b2a460/tests/test_blackdog_cli.py)
    - Updated direct calls to `_build_child_prompt` with new `protocol_command` parameter
    - Updated child prompt assertions to helper-based command paths
    - Adjusted inbox test to avoid env-dependent recipient filtering drift by passing explicit `--recipient supervisor` in resolved inbox listing

Why

- The child now gets a deterministic, workspace-local command entrypoint (`blackdog-child`) that bakes in task/project/agent context and chooses a usable Blackdog CLI path, eliminating repeated command probing and environment mistakes.
- CLI commands used by delegated children can now be less brittle by reading task/project/actor from environment, matching the new child run protocol.
- Prompt wording and contract metadata now explicitly steer children away from manual startup/claim/complete steps and toward the helper.

Validation performed

- Ran tests: `make test`
  - Result: `Ran 48 tests in 109.712s` `OK`
- Result recorded:
  - `cd /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog && PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m blackdog_cli result record --project-root /Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog --id BLACK-ed60a6b503 --actor supervisor/child-02 --status success --what-changed "Add child protocol helper wrapper and env-default CLI parsing for release/result/inbox list" --validation "make test"`
  - Result path emitted: `/Users/bullard/Work/Blackdog/.git/blackdog/task-results/BLACK-ed60a6b503/20260318-230044-db937cb4.json`

Residual risks / assumptions

- `cmd_inbox_list` now applies `BLACKDOG_AGENT_NAME` as default recipient when present; this is intentional for child runs but can hide messages for non-child sessions unless `--recipient` is explicitly set (tests adjusted accordingly).

  • result record: --id/--actor no longer required
  • Updated tests for new protocol behavior and prompt expectations:

Why

  • The child now gets a deterministic, workspace-local command entrypoint (blackdog-child) that bakes in task/project/agent context and chooses a usable Blackdog CLI path, eliminating repeated command probing and environment mistakes.
  • CLI commands used by delegated children can now be less brittle by reading task/project/actor from environment, matching the new child run protocol.
  • Prompt wording and contract metadata now explicitly steer children away from manual startup/claim/complete steps and toward the helper.

- Added an operator-facing fresh install section to [README.md](/Users/bullard/Work/.worktrees/wt-black-f83a97fecc-document-host-installation-paths-and-codex-skill-discovery-for-fresh-repos-33e6f5c3/README.md):

active_tasks.model_response:
Done. Task `BLACK-e982d3b588` has been completed and protocol-released.

1. What changed
- `src/blackdog/ui.py`: completed-task chip row is now wrapped in a `.result-chips` container for right alignment, and board snapshot now emits:
  - `generated_at`
  - `content_updated_at` (alias of generated time)
  - `last_checked_at` (derived from latest `supervisor_runs_dir/*/status.json`)
- `src/blackdog/ui.css`: added styling for `.result-top` and `.result-chips` to right-align completed outcome chips while preserving responsive behavior.
- `docs/FILE_FORMATS.md`: documented new snapshot fields `content_updated_at` and `last_checked_at`.
- `docs/CLI.md`: updated `blackdog snapshot` output contract to include both freshness fields.
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: noted freshness metadata in static snapshot payload.
- `tests/test_blackdog_cli.py`:
  - added assertions for new timestamp fields in snapshot contract
  - added regression test validating `last_checked_at` from supervisor status timestamps
  - updated static HTML text assertions to `"Last content updated"` / `"Last checked"`
  - added coverage for `class="result-chips"` in completed card rendering

2. Why
- Fixes the visual drift by right-justifying completed-task outcome chips.
- Makes board metadata trustworthy by exposing both content freshness and last supervisor check time in one snapshot payload and docs/tests.

3. Validation performed
- Task branch committed as `492dde6`.
- `make test` passed in this branch (`Ran 56 tests` with `OK`).
- Branch protocol output was recorded and released via child helper:
  - `blackdog-child result record --status success ...`
  - `blackdog-child release --note ...`  
    Returned task id: `BLACK-e982d3b588`.

4. Residual risks / assumptions
- `last_checked_at` currently picks the latest timestamp across all status files in `supervisor_runs_dir/*/status.json`; if non-supervisor actors write status files there, filtering may be needed later.
- `content_updated_at` is explicitly set equal to `generated_at` for now to preserve existing snapshot timing semantics while adding explicit naming.

grouping_guide.name: task
grouping_guide.meaning: The executable unit. Claims, results, completion, and dependencies are tracked at task level.

grouping_guide.name: epic
grouping_guide.meaning: The thematic why for related tasks. Epics organize reporting, not runnable order.

grouping_guide.name: lane
grouping_guide.meaning: A temporary ordered slot in the active execution map. Lanes are not executable state objects; they hold task order top to bottom.

grouping_guide.name: wave
grouping_guide.meaning: A temporary concurrency bucket for lanes. Waves are reused and compacted between runs; they are scheduler gates, not historical identities.
